commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=06b0b2f69e52f0461c0e0eb14e467eeac80b7597)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `audioAsVoice` and generated TTS filename checks to Telegram audio routing, plus a regression test for generated MP3 voice compatibility.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the generic media path: `ChannelOutboundContext` can contain `audioAsVoice`, while Telegram `sendMedia` currently does not pass that field to `sendMessageTelegram`. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor-visible code changes plus real Telegram behavior proof, so this should stay in normal review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: Fix Telegram TTS voice-note routing
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve generic `audioAsVoice` payload intent through Telegram sends
- treat generated TTS voice output filenames as Telegram voice-note intent when routing audio
- add regression coverage for generated TTS MP3 voice compatibility

## Verification
- locally verified OpenClaw Telegram delivery now logs `operation=sendVoice deliveryKind=voice` for Bernard tts-tool replies
- `openclaw config validate` passes after restart

## Context
Bernard/OpenClaw TTS tool replies can produce payloads with `audioAsVoice: true`, but the Telegram send layer only checked `asVoice`, so the request could degrade to `sendAudio`. This keeps explicit voice reply behavior working across generic reply delivery paths.
target.author: staysharp-rgb
